quizzes.title: General Knowledge
quizzes.description: Test your basic general knowledge with this fun and quick trivia quiz covering geography, science, and world culture.

questions.question: Capital of India?
questions.explanation: New Delhi is the official capital of the Republic of India.

questions.question: Largest planet in our solar system?
questions.explanation: Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system, with a mass more than two and a half times that of all the other planets combined.

questions.question: Which is the fastest land animal?
questions.explanation: The cheetah is the fastest land mammal, capable of reaching speeds up to 70 miles per hour (112 km/h) in short bursts.

questions.question: Which is the largest ocean on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest of Earth's oceanic divisions, extending from the Arctic Ocean in the north to the Southern Ocean in the south.

questions.question: What is the official currency of Japan?
questions.explanation: The Yen is the official currency of Japan. It is the third most traded currency in the foreign exchange market.

questions.question: Mount Everest is located in which country?
questions.explanation: Mount Everest, the earth's highest mountain above sea level, is located in the Himalayas on the border between Nepal and China (Tibet region).

questions.question: How many continents are there on Earth?
questions.explanation: Earth is divided into seven major continents: Asia, Africa, North America, South America, Antarctica, Europe, and Australia.

questions.question: H2O is the chemical formula for?
questions.explanation: H2O consists of two hydrogen atoms covalently bonded to a single oxygen atom, representing water.

questions.question: What is the national animal of India?
questions.explanation: The Royal Bengal Tiger is the national animal of India, symbolizing power, strength, and agility.

questions.question: In which direction does the Sun rise?
questions.explanation: The Sun rises in the east and sets in the west because the Earth rotates on its axis from west to east.

quizzes.title: React.js Basics
quizzes.description: Test your fundamentals of React.js, including hooks, JSX syntax, state management, props, and standard build tools.

questions.question: React is primarily maintained by which company?
questions.explanation: React was created by Jordan Walke, a software engineer at Meta (formerly Facebook), and is maintained by Meta and a community of developers.

questions.question: Which React hook is used to manage local state in a functional component?
questions.explanation: useState is a Hook that lets you add React state to functional components.

questions.question: What does JSX stand for in React?
questions.explanation: JSX stands for JavaScript XML. It allows us to write HTML elements in JavaScript and place them in the DOM without any createElement() methods.

questions.question: What is the default file extension used for React component files containing JSX?
questions.explanation: The .jsx extension is typically used to distinguish files containing JavaScript XML from standard .js files.

questions.question: Which library is the standard choice for handling navigation and routing in React?
questions.explanation: React Router is a fully-featured routing library for React, enabling single-page applications with dynamic, client-side routing.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the Virtual DOM in React?
questions.explanation: The Virtual DOM allows React to calculate minimal, highly optimized updates to the real DOM, boosting rendering speed and application performance.

questions.question: In React, component state should be treated as:
questions.explanation: React state should be treated as immutable. Mutating state directly can lead to unexpected UI rendering bugs and prevents proper state tracking.

questions.question: Which Hook is designed for handling side effects (such as fetching data or subscribing to events)?
questions.explanation: The useEffect Hook lets you perform side effects (data fetching, subscriptions, DOM manual changes) in functional components.

questions.question: Props passed to a React component are:
questions.explanation: Props are read-only (immutable) inputs to a component, ensuring a predictable unidirectional data flow from parent to child.

questions.question: Which modern, ultra-fast build tool is commonly used to scaffold React apps instead of webpack?
questions.explanation: Vite is a modern build tool that provides a faster and leaner development experience for modern web projects.

quizzes.title: Movies & Entertainment
quizzes.description: Dive into the world of cinema, wizarding worlds, and super heroes with this entertaining movies and media quiz.

questions.question: Iron Man belongs to which fictional universe?
questions.explanation: Iron Man is a key character created by Stan Lee under Marvel Comics, appearing in their comic books and the Marvel Cinematic Universe.

questions.question: Who directed the blockbuster movie 'Avatar'?
questions.explanation: Avatar was written, directed, and co-produced by James Cameron, known also for Titanic and Aliens.

questions.question: Who is the author of the Harry Potter book series?
questions.explanation: J.K. Rowling wrote the seven-book Harry Potter fantasy series published between 1997 and 2007.

questions.question: Who is the creator of the Sherlock Holmes detective character?
questions.explanation: Sir Arthur Conan Doyle was the British writer who created the renowned detective Sherlock Holmes in 1887.

questions.question: The famous Oscar Awards are presented for excellence in which field?
questions.explanation: The Academy Awards (famously known as the Oscars) are international awards given for artistic and technical merit in the film industry.

questions.question: Which movie features the ice queen character Elsa?
questions.explanation: Elsa, the Snow Queen of Arendelle, is a main character in Disney's hit animated feature Frozen.

questions.question: Batman is the protector of which fictional city?
questions.explanation: Gotham City is the fictional American city best known as the home of Batman in DC Comics.

questions.question: What main subject does Hogwarts School teach?
questions.explanation: Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry is the fictional boarding school for young wizards in Harry Potter.

questions.question: In which movie did the Minions first appear?
questions.explanation: Minions first appeared as sidekick characters in the 2010 computer-animated comedy film Despicable Me.

questions.question: The Joker is the iconic archenemy of which superhero?
questions.explanation: The Joker was introduced in Batman #1 (1940) as a maniacal criminal mastermind and Batman's greatest adversary.

quizzes.title: Science
quizzes.description: Challenge yourself with a hard-level science quiz covering chemistry, astronomy, anatomy, physics, and plant biology.

questions.question: What is the chemical symbol of Gold?
questions.explanation: Au is derived from 'aurum', the Latin word for gold, which translates to 'shining dawn'.

questions.question: Which is the nearest star to Earth?
questions.explanation: The Sun is the closest star to Earth, located at an average distance of about 93 million miles (150 million km).

questions.question: How many chambers are there in a human heart?
questions.explanation: The human heart has four chambers: the right atrium, the left atrium, the right ventricle, and the left ventricle.

questions.question: Which gas do plants primarily absorb during photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Plants take in Carbon Dioxide (CO2) from the air and release Oxygen (O2) as a byproduct during photosynthesis.

questions.question: What is the SI unit for the speed of light?
questions.explanation: The SI unit for speed or velocity is meters per second (m/s). The speed of light in a vacuum is exactly 299,792,458 m/s.

questions.question: The name DNA starts with which full form term?
questions.explanation: DNA stands for Deoxyribonucleic Acid, a molecule that carries the genetic instructions for all living organisms.

questions.question: What is the boiling point of pure water at sea level?
questions.explanation: Water boils at 100°C (212°F) under standard atmospheric pressure at sea level.

questions.question: Which planet is commonly known as the Red Planet?
questions.explanation: Mars is called the Red Planet because iron minerals in its soil oxidize (or rust), causing the soil and atmosphere to look red.

questions.question: Which is the largest organ in the human body?
questions.explanation: The skin is the body's largest organ, covering its entire external surface and accounting for about 16% of body weight.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of electric current?
questions.explanation: The ampere, often shortened to amp, is the base unit of electric current in the International System of Units (SI).

quizzes.title: Sports
quizzes.description: Test your sports expertise in soccer, tennis, basketball, chess, and traditional games like kabaddi.

questions.question: How many players are on the field for one football (soccer) team?
questions.explanation: A football match is played by two teams, each containing a maximum of 11 players including the goalkeeper.

questions.question: The Olympic Games are held every how many years?
questions.explanation: The Olympic Games (both summer and winter) are held once every four years.

questions.question: What wood is traditionally used to make professional cricket bats?
questions.explanation: Cricket bats are traditionally carved out of willow wood, specifically English Willow (Salix alba var. caerulea).

questions.question: Which sport is played in the NBA?
questions.explanation: The National Basketball Association (NBA) is a professional basketball league in North America.

questions.question: Which Tennis Grand Slam tournament is played in the UK?
questions.explanation: Wimbledon is the oldest tennis tournament in the world, held at the All England Club in London, UK since 1877.

questions.question: How many total chess pieces does each side start with in a game of chess?
questions.explanation: Each player starts the game with 16 pieces: 8 pawns, 2 rooks, 2 knights, 2 bishops, 1 queen, and 1 king.

questions.question: What is the badminton shuttlecock commonly called?
questions.explanation: A badminton shuttlecock is colloquially referred to as a 'birdie' due to it being made of feathers and flying like a bird.

questions.question: How many players are on court for each team during a Kabaddi match?
questions.explanation: In standard Kabaddi, each team has 7 players on the court at any one time during play.

questions.question: Which sport features the FIFA World Cup?
questions.explanation: The FIFA World Cup is the premier international association football (soccer) competition.

questions.question: Which format of cricket generally yields the highest total runs in a match?
questions.explanation: Test cricket is played over five days, allowing teams multiple innings to compile very high run totals (often exceeding 1,000 runs combined).
